danger_signals.visual_form: Player animation change, possible removal from ice

danger_signals.meaning: High-impact collision; physical consequence of aggressive play

safety_signals.visual_form: Puck control UI indicator, player highlight
safety_signals.meaning: Offensive advantage; ability to threaten goal

safety_signals.visual_form: Goalie on ice, ready stance animation

faction_indicators.color_identity: Primary jersey color (varies by NHL city)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: City name only (no NHL team logos due to NHLPA licensing limitation)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Away team rendered in contrasting jersey color
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Home arena ice with city-specific visual identity elements

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Away players rendered in lighter jersey
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Foreign arena; subtle visual distinction from home environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Single-game match setup; neutral playoff-style presentation
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: No cumulative game state; each match independent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Trophy presentation (Stanley Cup analogue) upon series victory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cumulative series tracking; elimination or advancement based on match outcomes

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lineup adjustments persist between matches (Genesis EEPROM save)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Team composition affects player statistics and on-ice performance

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of 24 NHL cities represented with distinct arena aesthetics; home/away jersey contrast establishes territorial identity
Real NHL player names and 1991–92 season rosters create historical continuity and recognizable team composition
Shattering glass on high-impact plays demonstrates realism; injury animations show tangible game consequences

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Responds to input; engages in skating, passing, shooting, checking
Removed from play or reduced performance capacity; visual/animation change indicates incapacity
Positioned in crease; responds to incoming shots with save animations
Pursues puck possession; attempts scoring; applies defensive pressure

notes: NHLPA Hockey '93 operates within constraints of NHLPA-only licensing (not NHL-licensed). This manifests in faction identity through city names rather than team logos/nicknames. The game communicates world-state through possession UI, score tracking, and playoff progression. Danger is signaled primarily through injury mechanics and glass-breaking collisions. The 24-team roster and real player names constitute environmental storytelling of the 1991–92 NHL season. No faction conflict or political narrative exists—the paradigm is competitive sport rather than world-building. Behavioral tells focus on player state (healthy/injured) and goaltender readiness rather than faction-specific traits.
